title: trigonella caerulea subsp. sativa
intro: Trigonella caerulea subsp. sativa, also known as wild fenugreek, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Trigonella caerulea subsp. sativa include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its antioxidant properties that may protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of certain diseases. Trigonella caerulea subsp. sativa is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or extract, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
